name: Eddie Palmieri
abstract: Eduardo Palmieri (December 15, 1936 – August 6, 2025) was an American musician, bandleader, and composer of Puerto Rican heritage. He was the founder of the bands La Perfecta (1961), La Perfecta II (2002), and Harlem River Drive (1970).

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Tito Puente and Eddie Palmieri. I did a concert at Carnegie Hall where they brought McCoy Tyner and Eddie Palmieri together and I played with that situation
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Janis Siegel
about_quotes.evidence: Bob was teacher to Eddie Palmieri... I learned about permutations... But I never took like Theory 101
about_quotes.doc_title: Janis Siegel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: professional percussionists that were playing with Eddie Palmieri in the 70s, and they seen that I had a little bit of talent so they took me up and cleaned me up and polished
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: And it was an Eddie Palmieri record. And he said, “listen to the bass player.” ... And I tried to do it. I played along with the record.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Daley
about_quotes.evidence: Latin music was very popular so you know the music of Tito Puente, you know, those type of, Eddie Palmieri and everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joseph Daley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 18, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: The label is Willie Colon and Eddie Palmieri. ... I bought an Eddie Palmieri “Azucar Pa Ti.” I still have them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025
